items.name: Ravenwake
items.identity: Control, exile, protection — magic through discipline. The blade that was not born noble, but became so in exile. Its meaning is earned through Rowan, not inherited from House Thornevale.
items.nextTrigger: Rowan protects someone when vengeance would be easier.
items.nextPowers: Reveals the name Ravenwake. Rowan may use it as a spellcasting focus. Gains charges equal to proficiency bonus — on a hit, spend 1 charge for +1d6 force damage. Charges return at dawn.
items.arcPurpose: Arc 3 spotlight — Ravenwake. Master blood, fear, shame, and violent inheritance. Break domination, inherited shame, or forced identity. Ascendant: once per long rest, when Rowan would drop to 0 HP, he drops to 1 HP instead and may move half speed without provoking opportunity attacks.
items.dmReveal: The sword sometimes feels warm after Rowan casts magic, but never during rage.
items.nextReveal: A dark shimmer crosses the blade like a raven's wing.

items.name: The Black Question
items.ownerClass: Carries a presence — whispers, acid, hunger
items.identity: Warning, evidence, acid, containment, dangerous knowledge. The staff that warns not only of danger, but of significance. Its grain occasionally resembles almost-letters.
items.currentPowers: Functions as a normal Quarterstaff of Warning. Grants advantage on initiative and warnings as per the item. Occasionally reacts to ruins, vessels, inscriptions, or old marks beyond ordinary danger.
items.nextTrigger: Xyrrath preserves evidence others would destroy.
items.nextPowers: Reveals the name The Black Question. Advantage on Intelligence checks to examine ancient inscriptions, magical residue, sealed vessels, unknown substances, or ruins. Once per long rest, cast identify without components on an object or substance he can physically touch.
items.arcPurpose: Arc 1 spotlight — The Black Question. Recognise and preserve dangerous truth. Understand something others would destroy or misread. Ascendant: once per long rest, while touching the staff to a ruin, relic, corpse, inscription, or unknown substance — ask one question. The DM answers with one true sensory or factual impression.
items.dmReveal: The staff warns of danger as expected, but sometimes reacts to ruins, sealed vessels, inscriptions, or old marks — things that are not threats, but are significant.
items.nextReveal: The staff does not speak. The Ashvein-dark grain seems to arrange itself into almost-letters.

items.name: Caeli's Vigil
items.identity: Boundary, grief, undead, true trails, holding the line. The bow that watches the line he could not hold. The gift becomes a vow — the name can emerge privately from Korvann rather than arriving pre-named.
items.currentPowers: Functions as a normal +1 longbow. The bowstring sometimes stirs in a wind no one else feels.
items.nextTrigger: Korvann protects someone from undead, shadow, or a false trail.
items.nextPowers: The bow reveals, or Korvann privately gives it, the name Caeli's Vigil. Once per turn, when Korvann hits an undead, shadow-touched creature, or creature marked by the corrupted rune, deal an extra 1d4 radiant or thunder damage.
items.arcPurpose: Arc 2 spotlight — Caeli's Vigil. Hold a boundary against the dead or shadow. Stop something escaping, crossing, or hiding. Ascendant: once per long rest, fire an arrow into any surface — a 30ft radius boundary forms for 1 minute. Allies inside have advantage on saves vs. frightened/possessed/charmed by undead. Undead treat the boundary as difficult terrain.
items.dmReveal: The bowstring sometimes stirs in a wind no one else feels.
items.nextReveal: The arrow lands with the sound of distant wind over stone.

items.name: The Oath-Ring of Veyr
items.form: Wondrous item — arm-ring (or torc, brooch, verse-stone, or horn-band)
items.found: To be given or found in the next session
items.identity: Names, oaths, memory, truth, witness. Recommended form: arm-ring, torc, or verse-stone — keeps the skald in a different lane from the weapon-bearers. The inscription is incomplete until spoken aloud.
items.currentPowers: Keeper of Names: advantage on checks to remember oral history, lineage, oath-forms, old songs, and culturally significant names. Word Before Steel: once per long rest, add 1d4 to any roll within 30ft (after the roll, before the result). The Ring Listens: when someone knowingly breaks an oath spoken while touching the ring, Ragnfridd feels a brief cold pressure — no details, only that the oath was broken.
items.nextTrigger: Ragnfridd uses verse to stop violence, reveal truth, or make someone keep faith.
items.nextPowers: The item reveals its name: The Oath-Ring of Veyr. Word Before Steel becomes usable a number of times equal to proficiency bonus per long rest. A new line of old verse appears where the metal was smooth.
items.arcPurpose: Arc 4 witness — The Oath-Ring of Veyr. The party becomes the instrument. Truth is named, the boundary is held, choice remains free. Ascendant: once per long rest, when an ally within 60ft fails a death save, saving throw, or key ability check — Ragnfridd speaks their name and one true thing about them. The roll is rerolled with advantage.
items.dmReveal: The inscription is incomplete until spoken aloud.
items.nextReveal: A line of old verse appears where the metal was smooth.
